lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Did you go to Uzer to see Badden?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: You must hurry!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: You idiot! You lost Silverlight! I found it on the sand!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: You need to take it with you and see Badden in Uzer.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Excuse me, I'm looking for [player name]. I have an urgent job for [him/her].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's me!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Thank Saradomin I found you!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: I believe you still have the magical sword Silverlight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The sword I used to defeat the demon Delrith? Yes, I still have it.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Now, do you recognise this sword?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Silverlight! That's the sword I used to defeat the demon Delrith!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: There is an urgent task that only you can do - and only with Silverlight!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: My partner Badden and I have been observing the dark wizard Denath, who we believe has been preparing to summon the powerful demon Agrith-Naar.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: A week ago he took a group of apprentice dark wizards and moved to Uzer, the lost city in the desert. We think he's going to use the city's ancient temple to summon the demon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to stop him?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Stop him? No! We want you to wait until Agrith-Naar is summoned and then kill him once and for all!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Let me explain. Agrith-Naar is not an ordinary demon. He has immense magical powers, and he can use these powers to influence the mortal plane.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: This summoning is our chance to rid the world of his evil influence!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: You should go to Uzer and find Badden at once. I'll follow once I've finished some business here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll let you know if I see [him/her].

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: It is good to see a friendly face here. The priest has said nothing to me but 'Get ye away, foul construct!'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why does Badden hate you?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I do not know. I heard him say that Saradomin hates all artificial creatures.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: It was not always so. When I was new, the priests of Saradomin praised the ingenuity of the golem-builders.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I paraded through the city with my siblings, all painted in bright colours, and they blessed us with holy water as we prepared for battle.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: He should know that I could never hurt him. The first command in a golem's mind is that it cannot allow a human being to come to harm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about the dark wizards who arrived here?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Some of them were curious about me when they arrived, but since then they have ignored me.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: As they passed me I felt a sense of dark foreboding, something I have not felt since the great enemy was nearby. I fear they are planning some great evil.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to go through the portal.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Go away! This is OUR portal now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Go away then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you get those robes?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Yeah, evil, aren't they? One of the group made them. He said he found some mushrooms in the ruins that could dye things really black.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: What brings you to this godsforsaken desert?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reen sent me.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Saradomin be praised! And you have Silverlight! You're willing to help us, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Agrith-Naar.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Agrith-Naar is not so much a demon as a force of nature. While he is in his own dimension his evil magics can reach everywhere in the world.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: No one knows how far his power extends, but Reen and I believe that much of the suffering in the world is due to him.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: The accident that cripples a man, the storm that sweeps a ship under the sea, the sudden fire in the blood that makes a wolf pack attack a town - all that is Agrith- Naar!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Denath.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: We've been keeping an eye on Denath for some years now, but we've never been able to move against him.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: He was part of the group that summoned Delrith, but he left Varrock before you arrived and defeated the demon.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: He's been studying Agrith-Naar for years, but when he brought his group here we knew he was about to make his move.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why has he come to Uzer?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: I'm not sure. This city is very ancient, and its builders were powerful workers of evil magic.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: As that foul construct over there proves.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Perhaps there's something here he needs. Do you know what happened here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Foul construct? Do you mean the golem?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: The golem, yes! An abomination in the eyes of holy Saradomin! A mockery of life! Right now Denath is more important, but once he is dealt with I should find some way to destroy it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the golem was created to fight a demon! You're on the same side!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thousands of years ago a demon attacked this city, but the golem army drove it off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When I last came here I opened the portal, and the demon was already dead - it had died of the wounds the golems inflicted.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: You opened a portal to a demon's realm? That must be why Denath is here!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: It would be much easier to reach Agrith-Naar's realm from another demon's realm than from the mortal plane.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Denath must have heard that the portal here was open and decided to make his move!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I came here and opened a portal to a demon's realm...

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: You opened a portal to a demon's realm? That must be why Denath is here!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: It would be much easier to reach Agrith-Naar's realm from another demon's realm than from the mortal plane.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Denath must have heard that the portal here was open and decided to make his move!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: You have to be in position to kill Agrith-Naar as soon as he is summoned. The best way to do that is to infiltrate the group that is summoning him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: I don't know. You'll have to find some way to convince them you're one of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd never take part in a demonic ritual!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Oh, don't be so simple-minded! You'll be summoning a demon in order to kill it, to rid the world of great evil! You won't really be joining the group, just infiltrating it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: I don't know. You'll have to find some way to convince them you're one of them.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Saradomin be praised!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: But you will need Silverlight for this task. You should go back and get it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just passing through.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Oh dear. I was hoping [player name] would arrive.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: How is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't managed to infiltrate the group yet.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Oh dear. Do try again. You must be in place when they summon the demon!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: How is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to get into the group.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Oh, that is good! Make sure you are in place when they summon the demon!

sections.title: Talking to Father Reen at Uzer

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Phew! It's too hot for me out here. But at least I found my way through the desert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why didn't you take the magic carpet?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Oh dear no, you won't get me up in one of those things! What if the magic failed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you change out of your heavy robes?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: But the rules of my order say I must wear them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps the rules were made in a colder place than this?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Saradomin's rules are once and for all! It is not our place to question them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, soon I'll have killed the demon and we can all go home!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Summoning a demon! Those wizards make me sick! What kind of sad people would call on a supernatural being to solve their problems for them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, isn't that like what you do when you pray?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: What? No, that's totally different, because... um... because we always give thanks to Saradomin, even when he doesn't do anything!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Not like those wizards! They're so full of themselves, with their fancy robes and their rituals!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um, isn't that like...

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: No, it's totally different!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Anyway, there must be something wrong with the way they live in their little group and separate themselves from the rest of society. It can't be healthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's *nothing* like you being a monk, of course.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: No, nothing like that.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Wait, are you being sarcastic? Because you should know that sarcasm is specifically forbidden in the holy book of Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure Saradomin was being serious about that bit?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Leave me alone! I am preparing for the summoning ritual. You should make yourself a demonic sigil and practice the incantation with the other wizards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I forgot the incantation.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Ignoramus! Are you incapable of even the simplest task? The incantation is Nahudu Camerinthum Caldar Agrith- Naar Tarren.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Welcome, sister! Soon we'll summon the great demon! Aren't we evil?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: And we've got such an evil throne room to do our ritual in!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: It's just a pity someone's removed the gems from the throne. That kind of vandalism is TOTALLY NOT EVIL.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Yeah, who would do a thing like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But isn't evil, you know, bad?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Yeah! We're BAD!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: No. Last I heard, Matthew had it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: I didn't see anything. He left last night, and Denath only posted me here this morning.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: You don't understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Understand what?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Nobody understands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What don't we understand?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Oh, it doesn't matter. You don't understand. Go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Huh. So that's all you want. Here it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Come back when you've got free space.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: I already gave that to you! Go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: He didn't understand!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't suppose he did.

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Agrith-Naar is going to make us all sooo rich!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah! We'll all be rich!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Yeah! I'm going to get myself a dragon longsword! And a million rune ores! And a small piece of coloured paper to put on my head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... if you like.

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Those gems on the demon's throne look valuable. I just wish I had some way to remove them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't it more satisfying to earn riches yourself?

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: I don't want to be satisfied, I want to be rich!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Right now I don't have anything. But soon, I will have everything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Denath says he ran away. I don't know why. Who would want to run away from what Agrith-Naar is going to do for us?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: They won't ignore me for much longer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Everyone!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: When I have demonic powers, they'll all have to pay attention to me!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: And SHE won't ignore me then. I'll be able to show her how I feel.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I will make everyone like me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: No. I gave it to Matthew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: He went away and left us. He probably didn't like me.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Don't ignore me!

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Soon, all will bow down before me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Agrith-Naar will make us the rulers of the world! I shall be a dark queen over all Gielinor! All shall love me and despair!

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Ha ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Ah ha ha ha ha ha!!

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: AAH HA HA HA HA HA HAAAAA!!!!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Look, we've told you not to do that. It makes everyone uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Hah! Carrying such humble implements is beneath me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: He was a fool who could not cope with power! When Agrith-Naar makes us rulers of continents, I will find him and crush him!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Hello. Are you Josef's replacement?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I suppose I am.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: It just goes to show how little Denath values us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Denath told us we would learn the secrets of demonic magic, but all we've done is memorize one incantation. And now he replaces poor Josef with the next person who comes along!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have the demonic sigil mould?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: No, I think Jennifer has it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Shh! Listen, Denath doesn't know this...

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: When we joined him, Denath told us we were going to learn all the secrets of demonic magic, but we still don't know anything.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: The others, they just think they're terribly evil, with their sigils and the incantation they learned by rote. But Josef and I wanted more - we wanted to know how it worked.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Josef was more curious than me. Last night, he secretly read a book that Denath was keeping from us. He didn't know I was awake, but I saw what happened.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: I saw him read it, and then his face turned white, and he took the book and ran through the portal.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: But Denath was waking up, and when he saw Josef was gone he went through the portal to look for him. A bit later Denath returned, without Josef or the book.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Josef didn't have money for the magic carpet, and he can't have got far in the desert at night with that heavy book.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Josef must have hidden the book somewhere in the ruins, before Denath followed him. If you could find it, we'd know what it was that frightened him. We'd know what Denath is really up to!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: How is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see anything happen last night?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: I was sleeping last night. I'm only human after all!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: But I had this odd dream, about fear and panic, and... fire...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: It is good to see a friendly face here. The priest has said nothing to me but 'Get ye away, foul construct!'

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I saw one of the men in dark robes run out of the temple. He looked very frightened and I feared for his safety.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: He was carrying a large book. He started to head out into the desert, but then turned back and hid the book in one of the broken kilns in which my kind were created long ago.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Then another man came out of the temple. He said magical words and threw a fireball at the first man, turning him to dust!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I should have saved him, but I was not fast enough to throw myself in the path of the fireball.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which kiln did he hide the book in?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I cannot remember. The shock of seeing a human being killed wiped the details of the incident from my memory.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Did you find that book?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: I really want to know what's in it. I think Denath is hiding something from us, but we won't know what unless we read that book!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Did you find that book?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. The golem saw Josef hide it in one of the broken kilns in the city.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Did the golem see what happened to Josef?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It said Denath killed him.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: I knew Denath was a dark wizard but I didn't think he'd kill one of his disciples.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Let me look at the book then.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: I don't understand. What's in here that Denath thought was worth killing over? Josef must have worked something out - but what?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: But look at the incantation in the book. The words are in the reverse order to the order that Denath taught us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The incantation in the book is backwards?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: More likely the book has the correct incantation and the one Denath taught us was backwards. But what does that mean?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: [Player name], I'm not happy about this ritual. I don't think Agrith-Naar is going to grant all our wishes, I think he's going to kill us all. But if I try to stop it, Denath will kill me like he did Josef!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry. I'm here to kill the demon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I need find a way to smuggle Silverlight in here. It's the only weapon that can defeat Agrith-Naar!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Thank goodness! I will try to stall the ritual until you have Silverlight. Hurry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is the demon-slaying sword Silverlight, the only weapon that can kill Agrith-Naar!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Thank goodness! I think Denath is nearly ready for the ritual. We must prepare!

sections.title: Talking to Matthew again after showing him the book

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Are you prepared for the ritual? You must be ready to slay Agrith-Naar when he appears.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: How is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think Denath is being honest with his wizards.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Of course he isn't! You expect an evil sorcerer to be honest with anyone?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to join your group.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Well we do need one more person now, since... um.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: But we're an evil group! To join, you have to be evil!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm evil!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: You don't look evil. You certainly don't dress evil!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm evil in disguise?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: There's no need for the disguise here! We're all evil!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm evil!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Yeah, I can see that. That outfit is totally evil!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: But what's that sword? It's far too shiny to be evil. We won't have any silver glowing swords in here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm evil!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Yeah, I can see that. That outfit is totally evil!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Okay, I'll take you through to see Denath.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Master! This person wants to join us!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Indeed? Well we are one wizard short since Josef ran away. Can you vouch for [him/her]?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Yeah! [He/She]'s totally evil!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Thank you, Dave.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: So you wish to join us? You have made a wise choice. When we summon the demon Agrith-Naar, he will grant all our worldly desires.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: An abyssal whip, a dragon chain mail? To conjure these items is trivial for Agrith-Naar.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: But you will not need such weapons or armour, for Agrith-Naar will grant you the power of life and death over all creatures!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: You shall have every wish granted, and everyone who has ever wronged you will burn in a hell of your own devising!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't this, you know, wrong?

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Right and wrong are for the weak! This is your chance to choose to be on the side of the strong, and have all your desires granted, while the weak are crushed beneath Agrith-Naar's demonic feet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know Agrith-Naar will do all this for us?

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: My binding spells will make Agrith-Naar my slave! You would be wise not to question my powers again, lest I turn the same spells on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did Josef run away?

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Obviously he was a coward who did not have the strength to abandon his old-fashioned morality. I hope you will not be a similar disappointment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: When I have finished preparing the magic circle, we will all recite the incantation that will summon Agrith-Naar.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: You must memorize the incantation carefully. It is Nahudu Camerinthum Caldar Agrith-Naar Tarren.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: You will also need to make yourself a demonic sigil to act as a focus of power. One of the group has the mould you need to make it.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Now let me continue with my preparations!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I'm not evil.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Well get out of our evil temple then!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Our evil moment is at hand! You should get to your place in the circle so the ritual can start!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: We'd better do the ritual!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: You ought to get to your place in the circle. Denath wants YOU to lead the ritual.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: This is it! Get to your place in the circle and say the words!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Get to your place in the circle and lead the ritual! I'm so excited!

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Get to your place in the circle and lead the ritual! I'm so excited!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: [Player name] will lead us in the incantation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 1]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 1]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 2]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 2]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 3]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 3]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 4]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 4]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 5]!

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 5!]

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: You fool! Do it again, and get it right!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Oh my gods!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: He disappeared!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Where'd he go?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Of course! How could we be so stupid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: What do you get when you perform a summoning ritual backwards?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! We've just helped him return to his own dimension!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: What was that?!

lines.speaker: Tanya
lines.text: Look! The portal's closing!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: I'm getting out of here!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: No, don't leave!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Who knows what Denath could do now?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We've got to perform the ritual again so you can kill Agrith-Naar. But we need eight people!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Quick, Snotra, you've got to get those three to come back! And you'd better take Denath's sigil and find one more person to help.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We've got to perform the ritual again, forwards this time, so you can kill Agrith-Naar. I'll prepare for it here while you find four more people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Hurry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand what happened!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Denath must have been Agrith-Naar all along!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Ellemar's book said that Agrith-Naar can only use his powerful magics from the demon plane.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Ellemar must have trapped him in human form to stop him using his magics. That must be what the last pages of the book said.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: So the reason he assembled us was to send him back to his own plane, where he would be much more powerful! That's why the ritual was backwards!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: He's already started to close the portal to trap us. Who knows what else he could do?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: But if we can perform the ritual forwards then you can kill him using Silverlight and end his evil magics once and for all!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: We should have known it was too good to be true.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: It's always the same, everyone want to use you. Even demons.

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: What are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: What are we going to do?!!!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: I guess none of us are going to get rich now. Let's do what needs to be done!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We need to get everyone back in here! We need four more people to perform the ritual!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We need to get everyone back in here! We need three more people to perform the ritual!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We'll be ready to perform the ritual soon, but you need to find two more people.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We're ready to perform the ritual. We just need one more person!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Eric's dead! Agrith-Naar killed him! That's EVIL!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: I mean, it's bad! In a BAD way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: The ceiling collapsed without warning! When I moved towards the staircase the walls started to shake.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: I'm scared of what will happen to me if I try to leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've got to get back to the throne room!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: But the portal is closing! We'll be trapped!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Our only hope is to summon Agrith-Naar again so I can kill him! But I need eight people.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: You can kill him?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: You'd better take this then. It was Eric's sigil. Everyone in the ritual will need one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By using 'evil' as a term of praise you have left yourself without a word to describe real evil!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: There's no time for that! Eric's DEAD!

sections.title: Talking to Evil Dave inside the throne room

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: That demon killed Eric! When are you going to start the ritual and slay him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Good heavens! I've never seen a weather like this before! It looks like a real sandstorm is coming up!

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Ali Morrisane's carpet fleet flies in any weather, though! Do you require our services?

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: What's happening? The wind suddenly picked up! It's a desert sandstorm!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! We've just sent him back to his own dimension!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Saradomin protect us! From his own dimension he'll be able to wreak havoc!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've got to help me summon him again, so I can slay him with Silverlight!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But first I'll need to get you a demonic sigil.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: All right, I'll wait here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll need to hold this sigil for the ritual.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: What's happening? This sandstorm came out of nowhere!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! Now he's back in his own dimension!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Oh no! What can we do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've got to help me perform the ritual again so I can kill him!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Oh! Oh dear! I don't think I could take part in a demonic ritual!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll need to get you a demonic sigil anyway.

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: A demonic sigil?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But isn't that what you and Badden asked me to do?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: But things are different for me! I'm a monk! What if my order found out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the wizards will all die! Now Agrith-Naar is in his own dimension he's killing them one by one!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: They should have known better than to join Denath in his evil schemes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, don't be so simple-minded! You'll be summoning a demon in order to kill it, to rid the world of great evil!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Well... all right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now take this demonic sigil, and hurry inside!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I sensed a great darkness, as of a demon revelling in its moment of triumph.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: And now this sandstorm appears, the likes of which I have not seen in three thousand years. What can it mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! We've accidentally helped him get back to his own dimension!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: It is a terrible thing for a demon to be victorious. I wish there was something I could do to rectify the situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could help me summon the demon again so I could kill him!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I cannot do that. It is forbidden for a golem to enter the portal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I really need you to!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: A golem cannot disobey the commands in its mind!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is it forbidden?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I do not know. I was not privy to the thoughts of my designers, and now they cannot be asked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could just reprogram you again.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: That is true. Perhaps if you opened my head and removed the instruction concerning the portal I would be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: But there must have been a reason why my builders imposed this restriction. I do not know what would happen to me if I went through the portal.

sections.title: Talking to the golem after reprogramming

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I sensed a great darkness, as of a demon revelling in its moment of triumph.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: And now this sandstorm appears, the likes of which I have not seen in three thousand years. What can it mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! We've accidentally helped him get back to his own dimension!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: It is a terrible thing for a demon to be victorious. I wish there was something I could do to rectify the situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could help me summon the demon again so I could kill him!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: What are we waiting for? What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get you a demonic sigil.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I will wait.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I sensed a great darkness, as of a demon revelling in its moment of triumph.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: And now this sandstorm appears, the likes of which I have not seen in three thousand years. What can it mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Denath was Agrith-Naar all along! We've accidentally helped him get back to his own dimension!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: It is a terrible thing for a demon to be victorious. I wish there was something I could do to rectify the situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could help me summon the demon again so I could kill him!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: What are we waiting for? What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take this demonic sigil and go through the portal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get you a demonic sigil.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I will wait.

sections.title: Talking to the golem inside the throne room

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Strength low...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: This realm... sapping strength... combat capability impaired...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: This must be... reason... portal forbidden...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just need your help with the ritual. Do you think you can do that?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Speech functions not impaired.

sections.title: Talking to Father Badden inside the throne room

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Let's get this ritual over with! I don't want to spend any more time with that golem than I have to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your problem with the golem? It doesn't mind you!

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: Bah!

sections.title: Talking to Father Reen inside the throne room

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: So Agrith-Naar was trapped in our dimension all along, and didn't have any influence until the wizards un- summoned him?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: I thought slaying him would make the world better, but now we're fighting to return it to how it was before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps we can't blame demons for everything that's wrong with the world. Sometimes it's just not that simple.

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: It is time for the ritual to commence!

lines.speaker: Denath
lines.text: Player, please take your place in the circle!

sections.title: Talking to everyone in the ritual

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Strength low...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: This realm... sapping strength... combat capability impaired...

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: We'd better get on with the ritual. Let's get this over with!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Hurry up with the ritual! That demon killed Eric!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: I guess none of us are going to get rich now. Let's do what needs to be done!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: You ought to get to the head of the circle and lead the incantation. Let's get this over with.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: What are we waiting for? You must lead the ritual and kill Agrith-Naar! We're all counting on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 1]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 1]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 2]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 2]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 3]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 3]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 4]...

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 4]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Word 5]!

lines.speaker: [Everyone]
lines.text: [Word 5!]

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: No, we need to perform the ritual forwards, as it appears in the book!

lines.speaker: Agrith Naar
lines.text: Matthew!

lines.speaker: Agrith Naar
lines.text: How dare you summon me?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Aaaargh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He didn't summon you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did!

lines.speaker: Agrith Naar
lines.text: Then prepare to die!

sections.title: Talking to everyone after summoning Agrith-Naar

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: So that's Agrith-Naar's true form! Quickly, slay him with Silverlight!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Strength low...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: This realm... sapping strength... combat capability impaired...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: This must be... reason... portal forbidden...

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: Help! Quickly, use Silverlight!

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Kill him, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: Help! Save me from the demon!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Help!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: We've got eight people now. Are you ready to summon Agrith-Naar and slay him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Where's your demonic sigil? You'll need that in order to lead the ritual.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Where is Silverlight? It's impossible to kill Agrith-Naar without that magic sword!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: *gulp* Okay, here we go...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Do hurry! We haven't got much time!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Demon is dead... regret... could not assist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, Golem, you've done enough demon- slaying!

lines.speaker: Patrick
lines.text: So Agrith-Naar was never going to do anything for us. I suppose I'll have to make my fortune the hard way!

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: So Agrith-Naar was trapped in our dimension all along, and didn't have any influence until the wizards un- summoned him?

lines.speaker: Father Reen
lines.text: I thought slaying him would make the world better, but we've just returned it to how it was before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps we can't blame demons for everything that's wrong with the world. Sometimes it's just not that simple.

lines.speaker: Father Badden
lines.text: I thought golems were servants of evil, but that one helped us to slay a demon. Perhaps golems aren't so bad after all!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: At least that's all over.

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: Thank goodness that's over!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodness?

lines.speaker: Evil Dave
lines.text: I'm not going to try to be evil any more. From now on I'm going to be good!
